[...] she lifted (saying to Šamaš):

[... ṣa]-⸢li-lu⸣

'[...] resting [...]?

[... il]-⸢lak⸣

'[...] he (Gilgameš) will go

o ii 66

[... dḫum-ba]-ba

'[...] Humbaba.

o ii 77

[... i-rak]-kab

'[...] he (Gilgameš) will ride.

o ii 88

[... i-tur]-ru

'[...] he comes back,

o ii 99

[... giš]⸢EREN⸣

'[...] cedar,

o ii 1010

[... i-nar]-⸢ru⸣

'[...] he kills,

[... li-ḫa-sis]-⸢ka⸣

'[...] may she (Aya) remind you (saying):

o ii 1414

[... pi-qid]-⸢su⸣

"[...] entrust him!

o ii 1515

[...] x-ma

"[...] ...."

o ii 1616

[...] x ⸢NI?⸣

'[...] ...

o ii 1717

[...] x SI ana x

'[...] ... to ...

o ii 1818

[...] x IR

'[...] ...

o ii 1919

[...] ⸢na⸣-ma-⸢ri⸣

'[...] brightening.

o ii 2020

[... a]-⸢ṣe⸣-e ša₂ ⸢bu-li₃⸣

'[...] the going out of the livestock,

o ii 2121

[...] x tu-ṣa-a ana ⸢KUR⸣

'[...] you came out for the land.

o ii 2222

[... i-nam-mi]-ru AN⸢u₂⸣

'[...], the heavens become bright,

o ii 2323

[...] ⸢ru⸣-uš-ša₂-⸢a-ka⸣

'[...] your redness.

o ii 2424

[...] x x x x x

'[...] ...

o ii 2525

[... ka]-⸢a⸣-[šu₂]

'[...] you.

o ii 2626

[... na]-⸢šak⸣-[ku]

'[...] I am offering to you

(traces of 2 lines)
Column iii
o iii 1o iii 1

⸢a-di⸣ [d]⸢GIŠ-gim₂⸣-maš ⸢il-la⸣-[ku] ana ⸢giš⸣[TIR] ⸢gišEREN⸣

(may Aya say to Šamaš:) "while Gilgameš goes to the forest of cedar,

o iii 22

⸢lu⸣-u₂ ar-ra-ka ⸢U₄⸣-MEŠ ⸢lu⸣-[u₂] ⸢ku-ra-aʾ GE₆⸣-MEŠ

"may the days be very long, may the nights be short,

o iii 33

⸢lu-u₂ rak⸣-sa qab-la-a-šu₂ ⸢lu⸣-[u₂] ⸢pe⸣-[ta-a pu-ri]-⸢da⸣-a-šu₂

"may his loins be girt, may his legs be in motion.

o iii 44

ina ⸢mu⸣-ši liš-⸢kun⸣ ka-ra-⸢ši nu⸣-bat-ti

"At night may he pitch a camp for an overnight stay,

o iii 55

nu-bat-ti x x x ⸢i⸣-ni-⸢il-lu⸣

"an overnight stay ... they will lie down."

o iii 66

ši-i a-a <i>-dur-ka ⸢da⸣-a ⸢kal-lat GALtu₂ li-ḫas-sis⸣-ka

'May she not be afraid of you, may Aya, the great bride, remind you.

o iii 77

ana u₄-me ša₂ dGIŠ-gim₂-maš den-⸢ki⸣-du₃ u ⸢dḫum-ba-ba KI a-ḫa-meš⸣ <in>-⸢nen⸣-mid

'On the day that Gilgameš, Enkidu, and Humbaba meet with one another,

o iii 88

⸢di-ka⸣-aš₂-šum-ma dUTU ana d⸢ḫum⸣-[ba]-⸢ba⸣ me-ḫe-[e] ⸢GAL⸣-MEŠ

'raise the great storm winds against him, O Šamaš, against Humbaba:

o iii 99

⸢TU₁₅⸣-1 TU₁₅-2 TU₁₅-3 TU₁₅-4 ⸢tu₁₅ziq-qa tu₁₅ziq⸣-[ziq]-qa

'south wind, north wind, east wind, west wind, blast, counterblast,

o iii 1010

tu₁₅ša₂-par!(MAR-)ziq-⸢qa IM.ḪUL tu₁₅⸣si-⸢mur-ra tu₁₅A₂⸣.SAG₃

'gale, destructive wind, typhoon, demon wind,

o iii 1111

⸢tu₁₅šu⸣-ru-⸢up⸣-pu-⸢u₂⸣ tu₁₅me-ḫu-u ⸢tu₁₅a-šam⸣-šu-tu₄

'icy blast, storm wind, dust storm.

o iii 1212

13 TU₁₅-MEŠ lit-bu-nim-ma ša₂ ⸢dḫum-ba-ba li⸣-ṭu-u pa-nu-šu₂

'May the 13 winds arise and Humbaba's face darken

o iii 1313

u₃ gišTUKUL ša₂ dGIŠ-gim₂-maš ⸢lik-šu-du⸣ d⸢ḫum⸣-ba-ba

'and the weapons of Gilgameš conquer Humbaba.

o iii 1414

iš-tu at-tu-u₂-ka x x-⸢ka it⸣-tap-⸢pa⸣-ḫu

'After your own ... has been lit,

o iii 1515

ina u₄-mi-šu-ma dUTU a-na ⸢šaḫ-ṭa? šu-kun?⸣ pa-ni-⸢ka⸣

'at that time, O Šamaš, turn your face to the reverent one!

o iii 1616

pa-ru-u₂-ka ḫa-an-ṭu-u₂-⸢tu li⸣-[x] x (x) x-⸢ka⸣

'May your swift mules [...] ... you,

o iii 1717

šub-tu₄ ne-eḫ-tu₄ ma-a-a-lu ḫi-pi₂⸢lu-u na-di?⸣-ka

'may a relaxing seat, a (gloss: break) bed, be set out for you.

o iii 1818

DINGIR-MEŠ ŠEŠ-MEŠ-ka nin-da-be₂-e ša₂ ⸢ta⸣-x x lid-⸢di⸣-nu-ka

'May the gods, your brothers, give you the food offerings that you ...,

o iii 1919

da-a kal-lat GALtu₂ ina ⸢TUG₂.SIG₂⸣-šu₂ eb-⸢be₂-ti⸣ IGI-ka lik-⸢pur⸣

'may Aya, the great bride, wipe your face with her clean garment-fringe.'

o iii 2020

f⸢ri-mat⸣—dNIN.⸢SUN₂ i-tur-ma⸣ ana IGI d⸢UTU u₂-ša₂-an-na-aʾ⸣ ur-tu₄

Rimat-Ninsun repeated (her) behest again before Šamaš:

o iii 2121

dUTU d⸢GIŠ-gim₂-maš⸣ ul DINGIR-MEŠ x x-ka

'O Šamaš, will Gilgameš not ... the gods?

o iii 2222

ul ⸢it⸣-[ti-ka] ⸢AN⸣e i-za-⸢az⸣-za

'Will he not share the heavens with you?

o iii 2323

ul ⸢it⸣-[ti x x] ⸢iz-za-zu⸣ NIG₂.⸢GIDRU⸣

'Will he not share a sceptre with [...]?

o iii 2424

ul ⸢it⸣-[ti x x] ⸢ABZU i⸣-me-eq

'Will he not become wise with [...] of the Apsu?

o iii 2525

ul ⸢it-ti⸣ [dir]-⸢ni⸣-ni ⸢UN-MEŠ ṣal⸣-mat ⸢SAG.DU i-be-el⸣

'Will he not rule the people, the black-headed ones, with Irnini?

o iii 2626

ul it-ti ⸢dNIN.GIŠ.ZI.DA⸣ ina ⸢KUR.NU.GI.A⸣ [uš]-⸢šab⸣

'Will he not dwell in the Land of No Return with Ningišzida?

o iii 2727

[lu-ub?-ni-šu]-⸢ma dUTU⸣ x [x] x A TI [x x]

'Let me make him, O Šamaš, ... [...] ... [...],

o iii 2828

[...] ⸢giš⸣[TIR] giš[EREN]

'[...] the forest of cedar.

o iii 2929

[...] x x [x] ⸢a⸣-a ⸢ik-šu⸣-[ud x x]

'[...] ... [...] may he not reach [...],

o iii 3030

[...] x ⸢DINGIRti-ka GAL⸣[ti?]

'[...] ... your great divinity.

o iii 3131

[...] x AN x [x (x)] x [...]

o iii 3232

[...] x x [x]

o iii 3333

[...] x x x x ⸢ki-ma⸣ UN-MEŠ-ma

'[...] ... like the very people,

o iii 3434

[...]-⸢a-ka⸣ x ki-ma ⸢ḫi-pi₂⸣bu-u₂

'[...] ... as (gloss: break) ...,

o iii 3535

ana x x x-⸢ra dḫum⸣-ba-ba tu-še-reb-šu₂

'you are making him enter the ... of Humbaba.'

o iii 3636

ul-tu ⸢fri-mat⸣—dNIN.SUN₂ ana dUTU id-di-nu ur-ti

After Rimat-Ninsun had given (her) behest to Šamaš,

Reverse
Column i
r ir i  (2 lines missing)
r i 33

[...] ⸢ṭe₃-e⸣-me

[...] intention, (Rimat-Ninsun saying to Enkidu):

r i 44

[...] ⸢at⸣-ta

'[...] you,

r i 55

[... ši-ir]-⸢ki⸣ ša₂ dGIŠ-gim₂-maš

'[...] the temple oblates of Gilgameš,

r i 66

[...] x-niš-⸢šu?⸣

'[...] ...,

r i 77

[... qa-aš₂]-⸢da⸣-a-ta kul-⸢ma-ša₂-a⸣-[ti]

'[...] qadištu-priestesses, kulmašītu-prostitutes.'

r i 88

[...] ana ti-ik-⸢ki⸣ den-ki-⸢du₃⸣

[...] onto Enkidu's neck, (Rimat-Ninsun saying to him):

r i 99

[...] ⸢il-qa-a li⸣-qu-⸢tu⸣

'[...] they (the priestesses) have taken the adopted child,

r i 1010

[...] u₂-⸢rab-ba⸣-a tar-bu-⸢ta⸣

'[...] they will rear the foster child.

r i 1111

[...] ⸢el-qa⸣-a ana DUMUu₂-tu

'[...] I have adopted,

r i 1212

[... li]-⸢dam⸣-me-eq-⸢šu⸣

'[...] may he (Gilgameš) treat him (Enkidu) with favour.

r i 1313

[...] x (x)

r i 1414

[...] x x

r i 1515

[...] ⸢a-na⸣ gišTIR ⸢giš⸣EREN

'[...] to the forest of cedar,

r i 1616

[... ku]-⸢ra⸣-a GE₆-MEŠ

'[...] (may) the nights be short,

r i 1717

[... pu]-⸢ri-da⸣-a-ka

'[...] your legs [...].

r i 1818

[... nu-bat]-ti

'[...] an overnight stay,

r i 1919

[... li]-⸢iṣ⸣-ṣu-⸢ur⸣

'[...] may he guard.'
